name: Phase1 public project status
public_state: active edge development with reviewed B3 VM evidence, B6 X200 marker evidence, public status promotion, Base1 B6 X200 marker checkpoint release note, and Fyr black_arts staged-candidate evidence published
important_boundary: Percentages are planning estimates, not production-readiness, security-hardening, live-update, or autonomous self-modification claims. Fyr black_arts has staged-candidate design, fixtures, operator visuals, source-wiring checklist, and runtime-stub handoff evidence, but the first safe runtime stub remains pending under issue #317. Installer readiness, recovery-complete status, hardening, release-candidate readiness, daily-driver readiness, broad hardware validation, live-system mutation, and autonomous promotion remain not claimed.

projects.name: Phase1 operator console
projects.state: usable edge console with guarded host access, VFS, dashboards, help UI, themes, learning, and tests
projects.next_milestone: wire safe Fyr black_arts staged runtime stubs while preserving non-live defaults

projects.name: Fyr native language
projects.state: seed language and toolchain surface exist with F3/F4/F5/F6 evidence, runtime-safety fixtures, standard-library contracts, Fyr-aware tab completion, and black_arts staged-candidate design/operator-visual/source-wiring handoff evidence
projects.next_milestone: implement the first safe fyr staged runtime stub from issue #317 without candidate writes, host commands, network access, validation execution, promotion, discard, or live-system changes

projects.name: Base1 secure host / OS track
projects.state: B2 focused test-suite evidence passed, reviewed B3 VM evidence is present, and the B6 X200 marker chain is published through evidence, checkpoint, public status, and release note; claim remains not_claimed
projects.next_milestone: continue B4 recovery validation and repeatable physical boot evidence while preserving installer, hardening, release-candidate, and daily-driver non-claims

projects.name: X200 / Libreboot hardware path
projects.state: X200 Linux-libre host generated reviewed B3 VM evidence and B6 marker evidence with phase1_marker_seen; the checkpoint and release note are published; repeatable physical boot validation remains separate
projects.next_milestone: capture repeatable physical boot evidence and keep emulator, USB, recovery, installer, and hardware-readiness claims separated

projects.name: Security and crypto policy
projects.state: trust model, crypto policy roadmap, provider registry, profile docs, config schema, and integrity checks are present
projects.next_milestone: move from documentation policy into scoped implementation only after tests and review evidence

projects.name: Website and public docs
projects.state: public site, status page, status JSON, badge endpoint, native GitHub Wiki, refreshed source wiki, organized docs, X200 evidence report, B6 checkpoint trail, Base1 B6 X200 release note, and Fyr black_arts public status trail are in place
projects.next_milestone: keep the public status synchronized with implementation evidence and non-claims

projects.name: Repository organization
projects.state: minimal root has 11 tracked files, 17 top-level folders, 0 unplanned root files, 0 tracked build files, and 0 root status duplicates
projects.next_milestone: keep generated artifacts out of Git and keep compatibility links clean as work lands

non_claims: Fyr black_arts staged-candidate evidence is fixture-backed and design/contract oriented; first safe runtime wiring remains pending under issue #317
B6 X200 marker checkpoint is present with phase1_marker_seen; non-claims remain in force
B6 X200 release note is published; installer, recovery-complete, hardening, release-candidate, daily-driver, and broad hardware-validation claims remain out of scope

current_public_report.title: Fyr black_arts staged-candidate evidence and Phase1/Base1 B6 X200 marker checkpoint report
current_public_report.summary: Fyr now has command and action-aware tab completion for `fyr`, `fyr run`, `fyr cat`, and the default `hello_hacker.fyr` VFS demo; Fyr black_arts also has staged-candidate design, fixture, visual-mode, checklist, and runtime-stub handoff evidence, while the B6 X200 marker chain remains published through raw evidence, checkpoint, public status, and Base1 checkpoint release note.
